def solve(nums1)
    # Separate numbers into Odd (O) and Even (E)
    O = nums1.select { |x| x % 2 != 0 }
    E = nums1.select { |x| x % 2 == 0 }
    
    # --- Check possibility for Target Parity P = 0 (All Even) ---
    # P=0 is possible if and only if there are no odd numbers in nums1,

def solve(nums1)
    # Separate numbers into Odd (O) and Even (E)
    O = nums1.select { |x| x % 2 != 0 }
    E = nums1.select { |x| x % 2 == 0 }
    
    # --- Check possibility for Target Parity P = 0 (All Even) ---
    # P=0 is possible if and only if there are no odd numbers in nums1,
    # because if there is an odd minimum element O_min, it cannot be converted
    # to an even number by subtracting another element from nums1.
    possible_even = O.empty?
    
    # --- Check possibility for Target Parity P = 1 (All Odd) ---
    possible_odd = false
    
    if !O.empty?
        # P=1 requires at least one odd number.
        O_min = O.min
        
        # For P=1 to be possible, every even number x in nums1 must be convertible to odd.
        # This requires subtracting an odd number y such that y < x.
        # Since O_min is the smallest odd number, if x > O_min, we can use O_min for subtraction.
        all_evens_convertible = E.all? { |x| x > O_min }
        
        if all_evens_convertible
            possible_odd = true
        end
    end
    
    return possible_even || possible_odd
end

def solve(nums1)
    # 1. Separate nums1 into O (odd numbers) and E (even numbers).
    O = []
    E = []
    for x in nums1
        if x % 2 == 0
            E << x
        else
            O << x
        end
    end

    # 2. Check P_possible_0 (Can all be Even?)
    # This is only possible if there are no odd numbers, because if the smallest odd number exists, 
    # it must remain odd, as subtraction requires a smaller odd number.
    p_possible_0 = O.empty?

    # 3. Check P_possible_1 (Can all be Odd?)
    p_possible_1 = false
    
    if !O.empty?
        # If there is at least one odd number, we can potentially aim for all odd.
        o_min = O.min
        
        # If there are no even numbers, all are already odd.
        if E.empty?
            p_possible_1 = true
        else
            # If there are even numbers, they must be convertible to odd.
            # Conversion requires subtracting an odd number smaller than the current even number.
            # Since o_min is the smallest odd number, we just check if every even number is larger than o_min.
            all_evens_convertible = true
            for x in E
                if x <= o_min
                    all_evens_convertible = false
                    break
                end
            end
            p_possible_1 = all_evens_convertible
        end
    end

    # 4. Return P_possible_0 OR P_possible_1
    return p_possible_0 or p_possible_1
end
